samples.xml_description:
Implement a module for parallel-to-serial conversion, where every four input bits are converted to a serial one bit output (from MSB to LSB). The output signal valid_out is set to 1 to indicate the availability of valid serial output. When valid_out = 1, the most significant bit of d is output, and the remaining three bits are output sequentially in the following 3 cycles. Clock signal used for synchronous operations. Reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive. 4-bit parallel data input. Valid signal indicating the availability of serial output. Serial output representing the converted data. Width of parallel data input. Count of cycles for serial output.

Initialize the counter (cnt) to 0 and valid signal (valid) to 0.

On each clock cycle:

If the reset signal (rst_n) is high, the register (cnt) is incremented by 1.

If the counter (cnt) is 3 (indicating the last bit of the parallel input), the module:

  • Updates the data register (data) with the parallel input (d).
  • Resets the counter (cnt) to 0.
  • Sets the valid signal (valid) to 1.

Otherwise, the module:

  • Increments the counter (cnt) by 1.
  • Sets the valid signal (valid) to 0.
  • Shifts the data register (data) one bit to the left, with the most significant bit shifted to the least significant bit.

The serial output (dout) is set to the most significant bit of the data register (data).
